marketState: neutral-to-bullish — hot-weather demand and stretched-short COT positioning offset by ample storage and bearish short-term technicals
summary: Weather is the standout signal: cooling demand is running +12.7% above the 5-year norm this week and +17.9% for days 8-16, with range position near the top of the 5-year band (0.87-0.90), a genuine bullish driver into peak summer. Seasonality is modestly supportive too — price already sits above its typical-year median and the odds of higher prices climb to 63% over 4 weeks approaching seasonally-strong September — but the 51pp reliability spread makes that a weak edge on its own. The fair-value model has front price only 'SLIGHTLY OVER' today (+$0.244) and 'FAIR' at every longer horizon, and notably the modeled fair value itself rises from $2.929 now to $3.118 by +21 days, consistent with the weather-driven demand story. COT positioning reinforces the bullish tilt: managed money is net short at just the 18th percentile of the last 5 years, a historically stretched level that has often preceded short-covering rallies. Against that, storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise, and technicals are bearish on nearly every short-term timeframe (5m through 1d, negative MACD, 50<200 EMA), so the near-term tape doesn't yet confirm the fundamental bull case.

longTerm.entry: $3.05-$3.20 (scale in near current front price, add on any dip toward next-month/fair-value levels)
longTerm.stop: $2.85 (below the now/plus7D fair-value midpoints and recent support)
longTerm.tp1: $3.45 (approaching upper edge of the +21D fair-value band, $3.618)
longTerm.tp2: $3.70 (extension toward winter-premium curve levels, beyond the Nov contract $3.262)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider scaling in half now, half on a dip
longTerm.rationale: Cooling-demand weather is running well above 5-year norms in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows near the top of their ranges, the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.929 to $3.118 over 21 days, and managed-money COT positioning is at an 18th-percentile 5y net-short extreme that historically favors mean-reversion higher into seasonally strong September.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close below $2.85, weather demand rolling back toward/below 5y normal, or repeated bearish storage surprises would invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk — countertrend vs. the bullish weather backdrop, size down
shortTerm.rationale: Front price is flagged 'SLIGHTLY OVER' spot fair value (+$0.244) while technicals are bearish across 5m-1d (negative MACD, 50<200 EMA, RSI in the low-40s with no oversold signal), favoring a near-term pullback toward the $2.93-$3.01 fair-value zone before any weather-driven leg higher resumes.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back above $3.32, or 1h/4h RSI/MACD turning bullish alongside further acceleration in the 1-7D weather demand read, would invalidate the short.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand +12.7% (1-7D) and +17.9% (8-16D) vs 5-year norm, near top of historical range
Managed-money COT net short at 18th percentile of 5 years — contrarian bullish squeeze risk
Fair-value model rising from $2.929 now to $3.118 by +21D while front price stays inside the band
Storage +175 Bcf vs 5-year average with a bearish weekly build surprise (+13 Bcf vs forecast)
Technicals bearish across nearly all short-term timeframes (negative MACD, 50<200 EMA)
risks: Seasonality reliability is LOW (51pp 10-90% spread) — the 1-2 week directional edge is close to a coin flip
Large storage surplus could keep pressuring price if summer cooling fails to draw it down
Steep winter contango (Jan27 +$1.20 vs front) leaves front-month pricing sensitive to any shift in the winter outlook
